reasoning: With the knowledge cutoff at October 26, 2025, there are only about two months remaining until the December 31, 2025 resolution date. While Keir Starmer's government has faced significant turbulence—including the resignation of Deputy PM Angela Rayner, poor polling, policy rebellions, and electoral setbacks like the loss of a by-election to Reform UK—he remains in power. Removing a Labour Prime Minister involves a complex internal process requiring the backing of 20% of Labour MPs. Given that Starmer has just survived the autumn party conference season without a formal leadership challenge, the traditional period for removing a UK political leader has passed, making it highly likely he will hold on until the next major electoral test in May 2026. Resignations of a PM with a massive parliamentary majority within 18 months of their election are historically extremely rare, and normally require a catastrophic sudden scandal. Factoring in the brief remaining timeline of 66 days and the lack of an active, formal leadership challenge, the probability of an announcement of his resignation or removal before year-end is very low.
